model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Tennessee holds a commanding statistical edge over SMU, ranking 15th nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with an adjusted efficiency margin of +26.06 compared to SMU's +18.11 at 42nd, a roughly 8-point gap driven primarily by Tennessee's elite defense (AdjD 95.0, 13th nationally) that should stifle SMU's offense even though the Mustangs rank 27th in adjusted offensive efficiency. SMU enters with significant health concerns as star guard BJ Edwards and forward Corey Washington are both questionable, and the Mustangs are coming off a play-in game that adds fatigue to an already compromised roster. While the 6/11 seed line is historically upset-prone, Tennessee's SEC-tested defense, 8-point efficiency margin advantage, and SMU's injury-depleted state make the Volunteers a comfortable favorite in this matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Tennessee vs SMU – Midwest 6/11 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Tennessee's elite defense and significant efficiency margin advantage, combined with SMU's injury concerns and play-in fatigue, make the Volunteers a strong favorite despite the historically upset-prone 6/11 seed line.
evidence: Tennessee ranks 15th in KenPom (AdjEM +26.06) with an elite defense (AdjD 95.0, 13th nationally)
SMU ranks 42nd in KenPom (AdjEM +18.11) with a mediocre defense (AdjD 104.8, 95th nationally)
Tennessee SRS 22.08 (17th) and SOS 11.99 (18th) reflect SEC-caliber competition vs SMU's 20-13 ACC record
SMU starters BJ Edwards and Corey Washington both questionable with injuries entering the tournament
SMU already played a play-in game against Miami (OH), adding fatigue to an injury-compromised roster
Tennessee lost Cade Phillips for the season (shoulder surgery) but maintains a roughly 8-point efficiency margin advantage

modelTrace.content:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile my analysis.

**Key findings:**

**Tennessee (6-seed):**
- KenPom: #15, AdjEM +26.06, AdjO 121.1 (35th), AdjD 95.0 (elite, top ~10)
- Torvik: #15, AdjOE 121.5 (33rd), AdjDE 95.5 (13th)
- Record: 22-11, SEC (11-7, 4th)
- SRS: 22.08 (17th), SOS: 11.99 (18th)
- AP Poll: 25th
- Injury: Cade Phillips (forward) out for season with shoulder surgery
- Rick Barnes, experienced coach

**SMU (11-seed, play-in winner):**
- KenPom: #42, AdjEM +18.11, AdjO 123.0 (27th), AdjD 104.8 (mediocre, ~95th)
- Torvik: #42, AdjOE 123.1 (26th), AdjDE 104.9 (95th)
- Record: 20-13, ACC
- Injuries: BJ Edwards (star guard) and Corey Washington (forward) both questionable
- Already played a play-in game (beat Miami OH)
- Late-season struggles attributed to Edwards' ankle injury

**Analysis:**
- Tennessee has a massive ~8-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage (+26.06 vs +18.11)
- Tennessee's elite defense (AdjD 95.0) vs SMU's good offense (AdjO 123.0) is the key matchup
- SMU's defense (AdjD 104.8) is mediocre and Tennessee's offense (AdjO 121.1) should exploit it
- SMU has injury concerns with two starters questionable
- SMU already played a play-in game, adding fatigue
- Tennessee's 22-11 record in the SEC reflects much tougher competition than SMU's 20-13 in the ACC
- The 6/11 line historically produces upsets ~37% of the time, but the quality gap here is wider than typical

SMU enters with significant health concerns as star guard BJ Edwards and forward Corey Washington are both questionable, and the Mustangs are coming off a play-in game that adds fatigue to an already compromised roster. While the 6/11 seed line is historically upset-prone, Tennessee's SEC-tested defense, 8-point efficiency margin advantage, and SMU's injury-depleted state make the Volunteers a comfortable favorite in this matchup."
